political.s: Olive Garden is owned by Darden Restaurants (NYSE: DRI), which maintains a registered PAC (C00108282) and engages in federal lobbying. Darden spent $510,000 on lobbying in 2025 and historically gave to candidates of both parties — e.g., $573,500 to federal candidates in the 2011–2012 cycle — with a bipartisan distribution. Darden has reported no significant outside spending in the 2024 election cycle per OpenSecrets.
political.amt: ~$510,000 lobbying (2025); historical PAC contributions ~$200K–$575K per cycle

charity.s: The Darden Foundation, Olive Garden's parent company's charitable arm, has awarded more than $110 million in grants to nonprofits since 1995. Key partners include Feeding America (over $20 million donated since 2010) and the American Red Cross. In fiscal 2024, a $2 million grant funded refrigerated trucks for 10 Feeding America food banks, and Darden's Harvest program has donated over 146 million pounds of surplus food.
charity.amt: ~$2M+ annually via Darden Foundation; $110M+ total since 1995
charity.focus: Hunger relief, food insecurity, disaster relief, community arts

environment.s: Darden publishes an annual Impact Report with third-party-validated GHG emissions data since 2022, and executed renewable energy contracts powering 79 restaurants via community solar. However, shareholders have repeatedly proposed emission-reduction targets that Darden has resisted, stating it is 'not willing to set goals if we don't know how and when they can be achieved.' In fiscal 2024, 97% of beef supply came from North America in areas without deforestation risk.

labor.s: Darden/Olive Garden has accumulated over $29 million in regulatory penalties since 2000 per Good Jobs First Violation Tracker, and has faced numerous wage-theft lawsuits including a 2024 California PAGA suit alleging unpaid overtime and missed meal breaks. A 'Dignity at Darden' campaign led by the Restaurant Opportunities Center targeted systemic wage violations, racial discrimination, and off-the-clock work practices. Glassdoor employees give compensation a 3.5/5 rating, and surveys show most hourly workers report never receiving raises.

dei.s: Darden has a longstanding public commitment to DEI, scoring 100/100 on the HRC Corporate Equality Index in 2013 as the only restaurant company to achieve that rating. The company maintains Employee Resource Groups for LGBTQ+, women, veterans, and other communities, and in fiscal 2024 provided a $500,000 grant to support anti-racism and equity training curricula. Darden also sponsors leadership development conferences focused on inclusion.

animals.s: As a restaurant chain, Olive Garden does not manufacture or sell consumer products subject to animal testing policies. Darden does maintain an Animal Welfare Policy for its food supply chain, employing PAACO-certified auditors and focusing on humane housing, avoidance of pain, and responsible antibiotic use in protein supply chains.

guns.s: Olive Garden is a casual dining restaurant chain and does not sell or manufacture firearms. The company has no disclosed firearms retail or manufacturing business. Darden has also publicly denied any political donations to presidential campaigns when rumors circulated in 2019.

privacy.s: Olive Garden/Darden collects customer data through digital ordering, loyalty programs, and online platforms, and is subject to standard U.S. data privacy regulations. The company has not received notable recognition from privacy-focused watchdogs, and a 2009 class action settlement involved improper display of credit card numbers on receipts. No comprehensive independent privacy audit from EFF or Mozilla is publicly available.

execPay.s: CEO Ricardo Cardenas received total compensation of $14 million in fiscal 2025, a 16.6% increase year-over-year, while the median Darden hourly worker earns roughly $16,000–$18,000 annually (per historical SEC proxy disclosures). This implies a CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio in the range of approximately 700–900:1, consistent with industry norms for large full-service restaurant operators. The restaurant industry median CEO pay ratio was ~283:1, making Darden's ratio significantly above average.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
